NAFLD Transcriptomic Analysis

Nicolas Legrand

18 juin 2026

0.1 1. Dataset loading and initial exploration

1 Load GEO dataset and inspect expression matrix + metadata

# ---- Setup ----
library(GEOquery)
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : Biobase
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : BiocGenerics
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : generics
## 
## Attachement du package : 'generics'
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:base':
## 
##     as.difftime, as.factor, as.ordered, intersect, is.element, setdiff,
##     setequal, union
## 
## Attachement du package : 'BiocGenerics'
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:stats':
## 
##     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:base':
## 
##     anyDuplicated, aperm, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind,
##     colnames, dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find,
##     get, grep, grepl, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget,
##     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank,
##     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, saveRDS, table, tapply, unique,
##     unsplit, which.max, which.min
## Welcome to Bioconductor
## 
##     Vignettes contain introductory material; view with
##     'browseVignettes()'. To cite Bioconductor, see
##     'citation("Biobase")', and for packages 'citation("pkgname")'.
## Setting options('download.file.method.GEOquery'='auto')
## Setting options('GEOquery.inmemory.gpl'=FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.1     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.3     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.2
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::combine()    masks Biobase::combine(), BiocGenerics::combine()
## ✖ dplyr::filter()     masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()        masks stats::lag()
## ✖ ggplot2::Position() masks BiocGenerics::Position(), base::Position()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
# ---- Download GEO dataset ----
gse <- getGEO("GSE135251", GSEMatrix = TRUE)
## Found 1 file(s)
## GSE135251_series_matrix.txt.gz
# Verify download
length(gse)
## [1] 1
names(gse)
## [1] "GSE135251_series_matrix.txt.gz"
expr <- exprs(gse[[1]])
meta <- pData(gse[[1]])

dim(expr)
## [1]   0 216
head(expr[,1:5])
##      GSM3998167 GSM3998168 GSM3998169 GSM3998170 GSM3998171
head(meta)

1.1 2. Download and extract raw count files

2 Retrieve supplementary files and extract raw featureCounts outputs

library(GEOquery)

# Télécharger les fichiers supplémentaires (RAW)
getGEOSuppFiles("GSE135251")
## Using locally cached version of supplementary file(s) GSE135251 found here:
## C:/Users/nicol/OneDrive/Documents/Metagenomics/GSE135251/GSE135251_RAW.tar
# Décompresser l’archive RAW dans un sous-dossier
untar("GSE135251/GSE135251_RAW.tar", exdir = "GSE135251/raw")

# Lister les fichiers disponibles
list.files("GSE135251/raw")

2.1 3. Merge raw count files into a single count matrix

3 Read all featureCounts files and merge them by Geneid

# ---- merge_counts_fixed ----

library(tidyverse)

files <- list.files("GSE135251/raw", full.names = TRUE)

read_count_file <- function(f) {
  df <- read.delim(f, header = TRUE)
  
  # Identify gene column (Geneid or first column)
  gene_col <- grep("Geneid|gene|Gene|ID", colnames(df), ignore.case = TRUE)
  if (length(gene_col) == 0) gene_col <- 1
  
  # Identify count column (usually second column)
  count_col <- grep("count|Counts|read", colnames(df), ignore.case = TRUE)
  if (length(count_col) == 0) count_col <- 2
  
  df <- df[, c(gene_col[1], count_col[1])]
  colnames(df) <- c("Geneid", basename(f))
  
  return(df)
}

# Read all files
count_list <- lapply(files, read_count_file)

# Merge all by Geneid
counts <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = "Geneid", all = TRUE), count_list)

# Replace NA by 0
counts[is.na(counts)] <- 0

# Set rownames
rownames(counts) <- counts$Geneid
counts <- counts[, -1]

# Check
dim(counts)
## [1] 64257   216
counts[1:5, 1:5]

3.1 4. Clean count matrix

4 Remove featureCounts summary rows and convert to integer matrix

# ---- clean_counts ----

# Remove featureCounts summary rows
bad_rows <- grep("^__", rownames(counts))
length(bad_rows)   # should be 5
## [1] 5
counts_clean <- counts[-bad_rows, ]

# Convert to integer matrix (DESeq2 requirement)
counts_clean <- as.matrix(counts_clean)
mode(counts_clean) <- "integer"

# Check again
dim(counts_clean)
## [1] 64252   216
counts_clean[1:5, 1:5]
##                 GSM3998167_017-Ann-Daly_S1.counts.txt.gz
## ENSG00000000005                                        0
## ENSG00000000419                                      605
## ENSG00000000457                                      315
## ENSG00000000460                                       92
## ENSG00000000938                                       96
##                 GSM3998168_018-Ann-Daly_S2.counts.txt.gz
## ENSG00000000005                                       14
## ENSG00000000419                                      525
## ENSG00000000457                                      330
## ENSG00000000460                                       89
## ENSG00000000938                                       91
##                 GSM3998169_029-Ann-Daly_S4.counts.txt.gz
## ENSG00000000005                                        0
## ENSG00000000419                                      709
## ENSG00000000457                                      329
## ENSG00000000460                                      115
## ENSG00000000938                                      292
##                 GSM3998170_030-Ann-Daly_S5.counts.txt.gz
## ENSG00000000005                                        0
## ENSG00000000419                                      671
## ENSG00000000457                                      418
## ENSG00000000460                                       97
## ENSG00000000938                                      115
##                 GSM3998171_031-Ann-Daly_S6.counts.txt.gz
## ENSG00000000005                                        0
## ENSG00000000419                                      869
## ENSG00000000457                                      348
## ENSG00000000460                                      108
## ENSG00000000938                                      144

4.1 5. Clean and format metadata

5 Select relevant columns and create the condition factor

# ---- clean_metadata ----

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# Start from meta
meta_clean <- meta %>%
  dplyr::select(`group in paper:ch1`, `fibrosis stage:ch1`, `nas score:ch1`)

# Rename columns for clarity
meta_clean <- meta_clean %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    group = `group in paper:ch1`,
    fibrosis_stage = `fibrosis stage:ch1`,
    nas_score = `nas score:ch1`
  )

# Extract condition from group
meta_clean$condition <- meta_clean$group

# Make condition a factor
meta_clean$condition <- factor(meta_clean$condition)

# Check
table(meta_clean$condition)
## 
##    control       NAFL NASH_F0-F1    NASH_F2    NASH_F3    NASH_F4 
##         10         51         34         53         54         14
head(meta_clean)
# ---- clean_colnames ----

# Extract GSM ID from filenames
clean_names <- colnames(counts_clean) %>%
  stringr::str_extract("GSM[0-9]+")

# Replace column names
colnames(counts_clean) <- clean_names

# Check
head(colnames(counts_clean))
## [1] "GSM3998167" "GSM3998168" "GSM3998169" "GSM3998170" "GSM3998171"
## [6] "GSM3998172"

5.1 6. Align metadata with count matrix

6 Ensure sample names match between counts and metadata

# ---- check_alignment2 ----

# Check again
all(colnames(counts_clean) == rownames(meta_clean))
## [1] TRUE
# Align metadata to counts
meta_aligned <- meta_clean[colnames(counts_clean), ]

# Check
all(colnames(counts_clean) == rownames(meta_aligned))
## [1] TRUE
head(meta_aligned)

6.1 7. Create DESeq2 dataset

7 Build DESeq2 object and filter low-count genes

# ---- create_dds ----

library(DESeq2)
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : S4Vectors
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : stats4
## 
## Attachement du package : 'S4Vectors'
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:lubridate':
## 
##     second, second<-
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     first, rename
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:utils':
## 
##     findMatches
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:base':
## 
##     expand.grid, I, unname
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : IRanges
## 
## Attachement du package : 'IRanges'
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:lubridate':
## 
##     %within%
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     collapse, desc, slice
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:purrr':
## 
##     reduce
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:grDevices':
## 
##     windows
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : GenomicRanges
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : Seqinfo
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : SummarizedExperiment
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : MatrixGenerics
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : matrixStats
## 
## Attachement du package : 'matrixStats'
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     count
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:Biobase':
## 
##     anyMissing, rowMedians
## 
## Attachement du package : 'MatrixGenerics'
## Les objets suivants sont masqués depuis 'package:matrixStats':
## 
##     colAlls, colAnyNAs, colAnys, colAvgsPerRowSet, colCollapse,
##     colCounts, colCummaxs, colCummins, colCumprods, colCumsums,
##     colDiffs, colIQRDiffs, colIQRs, colLogSumExps, colMadDiffs,
##     colMads, colMaxs, colMeans2, colMedians, colMins, colOrderStats,
##     colProds, colQuantiles, colRanges, colRanks, colSdDiffs, colSds,
##     colSums2, colTabulates, colVarDiffs, colVars, colWeightedMads,
##     colWeightedMeans, colWeightedMedians, colWeightedSds,
##     colWeightedVars, rowAlls, rowAnyNAs, rowAnys, rowAvgsPerColSet,
##     rowCollapse, rowCounts, rowCummaxs, rowCummins, rowCumprods,
##     rowCumsums, rowDiffs, rowIQRDiffs, rowIQRs, rowLogSumExps,
##     rowMadDiffs, rowMads, rowMaxs, rowMeans2, rowMedians, rowMins,
##     rowOrderStats, rowProds, rowQuantiles, rowRanges, rowRanks,
##     rowSdDiffs, rowSds, rowSums2, rowTabulates, rowVarDiffs, rowVars,
##     rowWeightedMads, rowWeightedMeans, rowWeightedMedians,
##     rowWeightedSds, rowWeightedVars
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:Biobase':
## 
##     rowMedians
# Build DESeq2 dataset
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = counts_clean,
  colData   = meta_aligned,
  design    = ~ condition
)
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
# Remove genes with very low counts
dds <- dds[rowSums(counts(dds)) > 10, ]

dds
## class: DESeqDataSet 
## dim: 32169 216 
## metadata(1): version
## assays(1): counts
## rownames(32169): ENSG00000000005 ENSG00000000419 ... ENSG00000280452
##   ENSG00000280453
## rowData names(0):
## colnames(216): GSM3998167 GSM3998168 ... GSM3998381 GSM3998382
## colData names(4): group fibrosis_stage nas_score condition

7.1 8. Differential expression model

8 Run DESeq2 and list available contrasts

# ---- run_deseq ----

# Run DESeq2
dds <- DESeq(dds)
## estimating size factors
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
## -- replacing outliers and refitting for 183 genes
## -- DESeq argument 'minReplicatesForReplace' = 7 
## -- original counts are preserved in counts(dds)
## estimating dispersions
## fitting model and testing
##   Note: levels of factors in the design contain characters other than
##   letters, numbers, '_' and '.'. It is recommended (but not required) to use
##   only letters, numbers, and delimiters '_' or '.', as these are safe characters
##   for column names in R. [This is a message, not a warning or an error]
# Extract results for all conditions vs reference
# By default, reference = first level of condition
resultsNames(dds)
## [1] "Intercept"                       "condition_NAFL_vs_control"      
## [3] "condition_NASH_F0.F1_vs_control" "condition_NASH_F2_vs_control"   
## [5] "condition_NASH_F3_vs_control"    "condition_NASH_F4_vs_control"
# ---- results_NAFL_vs_control ----

res_nafl <- results(dds, contrast = c("condition", "NAFL", "control"))
summary(res_nafl)
## 
## out of 32166 with nonzero total read count
## adjusted p-value < 0.1
## LFC > 0 (up)       : 4666, 15%
## LFC < 0 (down)     : 5580, 17%
## outliers [1]       : 0, 0%
## low counts [2]     : 6863, 21%
## (mean count < 0)
## [1] see 'cooksCutoff' argument of ?results
## [2] see 'independentFiltering' argument of ?results
# ---- results_NASH_F2_vs_F0F1 ----

res_f2 <- results(dds, contrast = c("condition", "NASH_F2", "NASH_F0-F1"))
summary(res_f2)
## 
## out of 32166 with nonzero total read count
## adjusted p-value < 0.1
## LFC > 0 (up)       : 227, 0.71%
## LFC < 0 (down)     : 57, 0.18%
## outliers [1]       : 0, 0%
## low counts [2]     : 11852, 37%
## (mean count < 2)
## [1] see 'cooksCutoff' argument of ?results
## [2] see 'independentFiltering' argument of ?results

8.1 9. Extract results for main contrast (NASH F2 vs F0–F1)

9 Annotate genes and classify DEGs

# ---- extract_results_F2_vs_F0F1 ----

library(tidyverse)

res_df <- as.data.frame(res_f2) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ensembl_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj))

# Add gene annotation
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
## Le chargement a nécessité le package : AnnotationDbi
## 
## Attachement du package : 'AnnotationDbi'
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     select
## 
library(AnnotationDbi)

gene_anno <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys = res_df$ensembl_id,
  keytype = "ENSEMBL",
  columns = c("ENSEMBL", "SYMBOL", "GENENAME", "ENTREZID")
) %>% distinct(ENSEMBL, .keep_all = TRUE)
## 'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns
res_ann <- res_df %>%
  left_join(gene_anno, by = c("ensembl_id" = "ENSEMBL")) %>%
  mutate(
    label = if_else(!is.na(SYMBOL), SYMBOL, ensembl_id),
    direction = case_when(
      padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange >  1 ~ "UP",
      padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "DOWN",
      TRUE ~ "NS"
    ),
    neg_log10_padj = -log10(padj)
  )

head(res_ann)
table(res_ann$direction)
## 
##  DOWN    NS    UP 
##     2 20288    27

9.1 10. PCA for global sample structure

10 Visualize sample clustering using VST-transformed data

# ---- vst_pca ----

vst_data <- vst(dds, blind = FALSE)

pca_data <- plotPCA(vst_data, intgroup = "condition", returnData = TRUE)
## using ntop=500 top features by variance
percentVar <- round(100 * attr(pca_data, "percentVar"), 1)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(pca_data, aes(PC1, PC2, color = condition)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA — NASH_F2 vs NASH_F0-F1",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", percentVar[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", percentVar[2], "%)")
  ) +
  theme_bw()

## 11. Sample-to-sample distance heatmap # Assess global similarity between samples

# ---- sample_distances ----

library(pheatmap)

dist_mat <- dist(t(assay(vst_data)))
dist_mat <- as.matrix(dist_mat)

pheatmap(
  dist_mat,
  annotation_col = meta_aligned["condition"],
  main = "Sample-to-sample distances"
)

## 12. Volcano plot (NASH F2 vs F0–F1) # Visualize significant DEGs

# ---- volcano_F2_vs_F0F1 ----

library(ggrepel)

ggplot(res_ann, aes(log2FoldChange, neg_log10_padj, color = direction)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(UP = "red", DOWN = "blue", NS = "grey")) +
  labs(
    title = "Volcano — NASH_F2 vs NASH_F0-F1",
    x = "log2 Fold Change",
    y = "-log10 adjusted p-value"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

10.1 13. Heatmap of top 50 DEGs

11 Highlight most significant genes in the contrast

# ---- heatmap_top50_F2_vs_F0F1 ----

top50 <- res_ann %>%
  filter(direction != "NS") %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  slice_head(n = 50) %>%
  pull(ensembl_id)

vst_mat <- assay(vst_data)[top50, ]
vst_mat <- t(scale(t(vst_mat)))

pheatmap(
  vst_mat,
  annotation_col = meta_aligned["condition"],
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  main = "Top 50 DEGs — NASH_F2 vs NASH_F0-F1"
)

## 14. GO enrichment analysis (F2 vs F0–F1) # Identify enriched biological processes

# ---- enrichment_F2_vs_F0F1 ----

library(clusterProfiler)
## 
## clusterProfiler v4.20.0 Learn more at https://yulab-smu.top/contribution-knowledge-mining/
## 
## Please cite:
## 
## S Xu, E Hu, Y Cai, Z Xie, X Luo, L Zhan, W Tang, Q Wang, B Liu, R Wang,
## W Xie, T Wu, L Xie, G Yu. Using clusterProfiler to characterize
## multiomics data. Nature Protocols. 2024, 19(11):3292-3320
## 
## Attachement du package : 'clusterProfiler'
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:AnnotationDbi':
## 
##     select
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:IRanges':
## 
##     slice
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:S4Vectors':
## 
##     rename
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:purrr':
## 
##     simplify
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter
library(enrichplot)
## enrichplot v1.32.0 Learn more at https://yulab-smu.top/contribution-knowledge-mining/
## 
## Please cite:
## 
## Guangchuang Yu, Li-Gen Wang, and Qing-Yu He. ChIPseeker: an
## R/Bioconductor package for ChIP peak annotation, comparison and
## visualization. Bioinformatics. 2015, 31(14):2382-2383
up_genes <- res_ann %>% filter(direction == "UP", !is.na(ENTREZID)) %>% pull(ENTREZID)
down_genes <- res_ann %>% filter(direction == "DOWN", !is.na(ENTREZID)) %>% pull(ENTREZID)
universe <- res_ann %>% filter(!is.na(ENTREZID)) %>% pull(ENTREZID)

go_up <- enrichGO(
  gene = up_genes,
  universe = universe,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  readable = TRUE
)

dotplot(go_up, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle("GO — Upregulated (F2 vs F0-F1)")

### Recap — NASH F2 vs F0–F1

  • DEGs: 27 upregulated, 2 downregulated
  • PCA: low variance explained (≈14% + 10%), consistent with subtle biological transition
  • Volcano: few significant genes, but clear immune-related hits
  • Heatmap: moderate separation, expected due to patient heterogeneity
  • GO enrichment: strong immune signature
    • Response to bacteria (GeneRatio ≈ 0.275)
    • Antimicrobial humoral response (≈0.225)
    • Chemotaxis (≈0.225)

Interpretation: Early fibrotic activation driven by immune recruitment and antimicrobial signaling.

11.1 15. Extract results for secondary contrast (NAFL vs Control)

12 Annotate genes and classify DEGs

# ---- extract_results_NAFL_vs_control ----

library(tidyverse)

res_df_nafl <- as.data.frame(res_nafl) %>%
  rownames_to_column("ensembl_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj))

# Annotation
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(AnnotationDbi)

gene_anno <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys = res_df_nafl$ensembl_id,
  keytype = "ENSEMBL",
  columns = c("ENSEMBL", "SYMBOL", "GENENAME", "ENTREZID")
) %>% distinct(ENSEMBL, .keep_all = TRUE)
## 'select()' returned 1:many mapping between keys and columns
res_nafl_ann <- res_df_nafl %>%
  left_join(gene_anno, by = c("ensembl_id" = "ENSEMBL")) %>%
  mutate(
    label = if_else(!is.na(SYMBOL), SYMBOL, ensembl_id),
    direction = case_when(
      padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange >  1 ~ "UP",
      padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "DOWN",
      TRUE ~ "NS"
    ),
    neg_log10_padj = -log10(padj)
  )

table(res_nafl_ann$direction)
## 
##  DOWN    NS    UP 
##  1838 22366  1102

12.1 16. Volcano plot (NAFL vs Control)

13 Visualize large-scale metabolic dysregulation

# ---- volcano_NAFL_vs_control ----

library(ggrepel)

ggplot(res_nafl_ann, aes(log2FoldChange, neg_log10_padj, color = direction)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(UP = "red", DOWN = "blue", NS = "grey")) +
  labs(
    title = "Volcano — NAFL vs control",
    x = "log2 Fold Change",
    y = "-log10 adjusted p-value"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

## 17. Heatmap of top 50 DEGs (NAFL vs Control) # Show major metabolic gene shifts

# ---- heatmap_top50_NAFL_vs_control ----

top50_nafl <- res_nafl_ann %>%
  filter(direction != "NS") %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  slice_head(n = 50) %>%
  pull(ensembl_id)

vst_mat <- assay(vst_data)[top50_nafl, ]
vst_mat <- t(scale(t(vst_mat)))

pheatmap(
  vst_mat,
  annotation_col = meta_aligned["condition"],
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  main = "Top 50 DEGs — NAFL vs control"
)

## 18. GO enrichment analysis (NAFL vs Control) # Identify metabolic pathways altered in NAFL

# ---- enrichment_NAFL_vs_control ----

library(clusterProfiler)
library(enrichplot)

up_nafl <- res_nafl_ann %>% filter(direction == "UP", !is.na(ENTREZID)) %>% pull(ENTREZID)
down_nafl <- res_nafl_ann %>% filter(direction == "DOWN", !is.na(ENTREZID)) %>% pull(ENTREZID)
universe_nafl <- res_nafl_ann %>% filter(!is.na(ENTREZID)) %>% pull(ENTREZID)

go_up_nafl <- enrichGO(
  gene = up_nafl,
  universe = universe_nafl,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  readable = TRUE
)

dotplot(go_up_nafl, showCategory = 20) + ggtitle("GO — Upregulated (NAFL vs control)")

### Recap — NAFL vs Control

  • DEGs: 1102 upregulated, 1838 downregulated
  • Volcano: dense and highly significant metabolic signal
  • Heatmap: strong separation between control and NAFL
  • GO enrichment: metabolic reprogramming
    • Lipid metabolic regulation (≈25/30 genes, GeneRatio ≈ 0.045)
    • Cholesterol biosynthesis (≈10/15 genes, GeneRatio ≈ 0.015)
    • Sterol/secondary alcohol biosynthesis (≈10/15 genes, GeneRatio ≈ 0.015)

Interpretation: Massive metabolic shift consistent with early NAFLD.

14 Final Summary

14.1 Two-step model of NAFLD progression

  1. Metabolic dysregulation (NAFL stage)
    • Large number of DEGs
    • Strong enrichment for lipid and cholesterol metabolism
    • Clear separation between healthy and NAFL samples
  2. Immune activation (transition to fibrotic NASH)
    • Few DEGs but highly specific pathways
    • Antimicrobial response, chemotaxis, humoral immunity
    • Matches early fibrogenesis described in the literature

14.2 Visual Recap Table

# ---- summary_table ----
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
## 
## Attachement du package : 'kableExtra'
## L'objet suivant est masqué depuis 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     group_rows
summary_df <- data.frame(
  Contrast = c("NAFL vs Control", "NASH F2 vs F0-F1"),
  Up_DEGs = c(1102, 27),
  Down_DEGs = c(1838, 2),
  Main_Theme = c("Metabolic reprogramming", "Immune activation"),
  Key_Pathways = c(
    "Lipid metabolism, cholesterol biosynthesis",
    "Antimicrobial response, chemotaxis"
  )
)

kable(summary_df, format = "html", caption = "Summary of Differential Expression Results") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))
Summary of Differential Expression Results
Contrast Up_DEGs Down_DEGs Main_Theme Key_Pathways
NAFL vs Control 1102 1838 Metabolic reprogramming Lipid metabolism, cholesterol biosynthesis
NASH F2 vs F0-F1 27 2 Immune activation Antimicrobial response, chemotaxis 
# ---- summary_plot ----
library(ggplot2)

summary_plot_df <- data.frame(
  Contrast = c("NAFL vs Control", "NASH F2 vs F0-F1"),
  Up = c(1102, 27),
  Down = c(1838, 2)
)

summary_plot_df_long <- summary_plot_df %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c("Up", "Down"), names_to = "Direction", values_to = "Count")

ggplot(summary_plot_df_long, aes(x = Contrast, y = Count, fill = Direction)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Up" = "red", "Down" = "blue")) +
  labs(
    title = "Differentially Expressed Genes per Contrast",
    y = "Number of DEGs",
    x = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
